title: Cheilanthes perlanata
taxonomy: Cheilanthes perlanata is classified as a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae. This family is a part of the larger order of Polypodiales and class Polypodiopsida. Within the family Pteridaceae, Cheilanthes perlanata is further classified as a member of the genus Cheilanthes. The genus Cheilanthes is known to comprise several species of ferns, often referred to as lip ferns or rock ferns. These ferns are typically found in dry, rocky areas and are known for their ability to thrive in arid environments.
uses: Cheilanthes perlanata, also known as the mountain lip fern, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for various purposes.
